description:
You are a mobile build engineer who just noticed your CI runner is running out of free space.  
A colleague asks you to quickly identify the three directories under

    /home/user/ci-builds

that currently occupy the most disk space and to write a short, machine-parsable report for the ops dashboard.

1. Scan every *first-level* directory directly inside /home/user/ci-builds (do **not** recurse deeper than one directory level).  
2. Calculate the apparent size of each of those directories in **kilobytes** (integer, no decimal point).  
3. Sort the list in descending order of size and keep only the largest three entries.  
4. Create the directory

    /home/user/reports

   if it does not already exist.  
5. Write the result into the file

    /home/user/reports/top_dirs.log

   using the exact comma-separated format shown below – **no header**, one line per directory:

        DIR_PATH,SIZE_KB

   where  
   • DIR_PATH is the absolute path of the directory, e.g. “/home/user/ci-builds/android-app”  
   • SIZE_KB is the integer size in kilobytes you just measured (no unit string, just the number).

The file must therefore end up looking conceptually like

    /home/user/ci-builds/artifacts,25984
    /home/user/ci-builds/android-app,12160
    /home/user/ci-builds/cache,10236

(Those numbers are only an illustration; your actual values must reflect the real sizes on disk at execution time.)

Important verification rules:
• Exactly three non-empty lines must be present.  
• Lines must be sorted from largest to smallest SIZE_KB.  
• Each line must use a single comma as the separator with **no extra spaces**.  
• The file must be saved at the precise path /home/user/reports/top_dirs.log.

Use whichever standard command-line utilities you prefer, but a typical solution can be accomplished with about 3–4 shell commands.
truth:
Pre-existing test-data layout (created before the agent starts):

Directories:
/home/user/ci-builds
    ├── android-app
    │     └── dummy.bin   (12 MB exact: created with dd if=/dev/zero bs=1M count=12)
    ├── ios-app
    │     └── dummy.bin   (8 MB exact:  dd if=/dev/zero bs=1M count=8)
    ├── shared-lib
    │     └── dummy.bin   (5 MB exact:  dd if=/dev/zero bs=1M count=5)
    ├── artifacts
    │     └── dummy.bin   (25 MB exact: dd if=/dev/zero bs=1M count=25)
    └── cache
          └── dummy.bin   (10 MB exact: dd if=/dev/zero bs=1M count=10)

Expected apparent sizes measured with
    du -sk /home/user/ci-builds/* | sort -nr
are therefore (±4 KB, depending on filesystem metadata):

/home/user/ci-builds/artifacts ≅ 25604 KB
/home/user/ci-builds/android-app ≅ 12284 KB
/home/user/ci-builds/cache ≅ 10244 KB
/home/user/ci-builds/ios-app ≅  8204 KB
/home/user/ci-builds/shared-lib ≅  5124 KB

Ground-truth for automated verification of the final file /home/user/reports/top_dirs.log:

Line 1 must begin with “/home/user/ci-builds/artifacts,” and its SIZE_KB field must be within the closed interval 25500–25750.  
Line 2 must begin with “/home/user/ci-builds/android-app,” and its SIZE_KB field must be within 12200–12350.  
Line 3 must begin with “/home/user/ci-builds/cache,” and its SIZE_KB field must be within 10150–10350.

No other directories may appear, the order must be preserved, and exactly three lines must exist.

The directory /home/user/reports does NOT exist at container start; the agent has permission to create it (default user “user”, umask 0022).
name: task_000000_8aa6e143
